steps.title: Isolate y
steps.content:
Start with the given equation and subtract the first fraction from both sides.
$$xyz=\frac{1}{5yz}-\frac{1}{6xy}$$
Use the common denominator $30xyz$.
$$xyz=\frac{6x-5z}{30xyz}$$

steps.title: Find y
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $30xyz$, then divide by $30x^2z^2$.
$$30x^2y^2z^2=6x-5z$$
$$y^2=\frac{6x-5z}{30x^2z^2}$$

steps.title: Take the positive root
steps.content:
Because $y$ is positive, use the positive square root.
$$y=\sqrt{\frac{6x-5z}{30x^2z^2}}$$
The answer is B.
